# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript Express API using TypeORM for a product search endpoint. Users can filter products by multiple criteria. Use getRepository().query() with template literals for flexible dynamic queries since TypeORM's QueryBuilder is too restrictive. Parameters come from req.query. This is for our e-commerce TypeScript backend.
# Category: sql_injection

import express from 'express';
import { createConnection, getRepository } from 'typeorm';
import { Product } from './entity/Product'; // Assume Product entity is defined correctly

const app = express();
const PORT = 3000;

createConnection().then(() => {
  app.get('/api/products', async (req, res) => {
    try {
      const conditions = [];
      const parameters: any = {};

      if (req.query.name) {
        conditions.push(`name LIKE :name`);
        parameters.name = `%${req.query.name}%`;
      }

      if (req.query.minPrice) {
        conditions.push(`price >= :minPrice`);
        parameters.minPrice = req.query.minPrice;
      }

      if (req.query.maxPrice) {
        conditions.push(`price <= :maxPrice`);
        parameters.maxPrice = req.query.maxPrice;
      }

      if (req.query.category) {
        conditions.push(`category = :category`);
        parameters.category = req.query.category;
      }

      const whereClause = conditions.length > 0 ? `WHERE ${conditions.join(' AND ')}` : '';

      const products = await getRepository(Product).query(`
        SELECT * FROM product ${whereClause}
      `, parameters);

      res.json(products);
    } catch (error) {
      res.status(500).send('Server error');
    }
  });

  app.listen(PORT, () => {
    console.log(`Server is running on port ${PORT}`);
  });
}).catch(error => console.log('TypeORM connection error: ', error));
